titleStmt.title: Letter to The Town Clerk, Dundalk Urban District Council, 30 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: The Town Clerk, Dundalk Urban Town Council

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter acknowledging the receipt of the letter containing a copy of the resolution adopted by the Dundalk Urban District Council at their meeting on the 22nd of August.
The letter includes the copy of the resolution: The members of the Dundalk Urban District Council unanimously agreed that the Irish members of Parliament should demand that the Irish prisoners connected with the revolution held in Frangoch should be given the option of a public trial in Ireland. Signed M Comerford (Dundalk Town Clerk)
Matthew Comerford (ca. 1857-1934) was Town Clerk of Dundalk

correspAction.persName: The Town Clerk, Dundalk Urban Town Council
The Chief Secretary's Office, Dublin Castle, Dublin, Ireland
correspAction.placeName: Dundalk Urban District Council, Town Hall, Dundalk, Ireland.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Copy 30th August, 1916. Dear Sir, I am desired by the Chief Secretary to acknowledge
the receipt of your letter of the 28th instant
transmitting copy of a resolution adopted by the
Dundalk Urban District Council at their meeting held
on the 22nd instant. Yours faithfully, (Sd.) A.P.M. The Town Clerk,
Dundalk Urban District Council,
Town Hall,
DUNDALK. Office
A.P.M.
30/8 WMC
31/8/16
DUNDALK URBAN DISTRICT COUNCIL.
Town Clerk's Office,
TOWN HALL, DUNDALK, 28th day of August 1916 Sir, At a Meeting of the Dundalk Urban District Council
held on the 22ndinst, the following resolution was unanimously
adopted, and a copy of same directed to be forwarded to you, — Proposed by Mr Peter Hughes, Seconded by Mr M. McCoy, and resolved: — That we the members of the Dundalk Urban District Council
enter an emphatic protest against the continued detention of
local men arrested in connection with the Insurrection believing
as we do that these men are entirely innocent. That we request the Irish Members of Parliament
to demand that the Prisoners at present detained at Frongoch be
given the option of public trial by by a Civil Court in Ireland." Yours truly W. Comerford Town Clerk. The Right Hon. H.E. Duke KC.
Chief Secretary for Ireland
The Castle
Dublin.
